senses.definition: to announce something again or publicly after previously announcing it
examples: The company will reannounce its new product next week.
They reannounce the dates of the tour after a scheduling change.

senses.definition: past tense of reannounce; announced again
examples: The spokesperson reannounced the new launch date yesterday.
They reannounced their commitment to the project after the delay.

senses.definition: the act of announcing something again or publicly reiterating information
examples: The reannouncement clarified the previously stated timeline.
There was a reannouncement of the event details.

senses.definition: present participle of reannounce; announcing again
examples: The team is reannouncing the dates after the venue change.
They are reannouncing the policy to ensure everyone is informed.

senses.definition: to annoy again; to cause renewed irritation
examples: The loud construction reannoyed the residents.
His constant interruptions served to reannoy the crowd.

senses.definition: the act of annoying again; renewed irritation or bother
examples: The constant delays caused great reannoyance among commuters.
There was notable reannoyance after the changes were announced.

senses.definition: to apply oil, lotion, or a substance again, or to consecrate again
examples: They needed to reanoint the statue with fragrant oil.
The priest prepared to reanoint the altar before the ceremony.

senses.definition: past tense of reanoint; anointed again
examples: The reanointed oil symbolized renewed blessing.
The hands were reanointed by the ceremonial leader.

senses.definition: the act of applying oil or consecrating again
examples: The reanointing took place during the ceremony.
A reanointing ritual is part of the tradition.

senses.definition: third person singular of reanoint; applies oil or consecration again
examples: They reanoints the relics as part of the ceremony.
The ritual reanoints the statue to bless it anew.

senses.definition: plural of 'rean' without common meaning; not a standard lexical item; likely a truncation or error
examples: Note: 'reans' is not a recognized standalone word in contemporary English.

senses.definition: to answer again or provide another answer
examples: Please reanswer the question with more detail.
If the time ran out, you can reanswer on the second sheet.

senses.definition: past tense of reanswer; answered again
examples: She reanswered all the questions after reviewing her notes.
The teacher accepted his reanswered submission.

senses.definition: present participle of reanswer; answering again
examples: She is reanswering the queries from the audience.
They spent the afternoon reanswering exam questions.

senses.definition: answers provided again; multiple revised responses
examples: The reanswers were graded for accuracy.
They submitted several reanswers to the reviewer.

senses.definition: to cause to become more antagonistic again; to renew hostility
examples: The debate could reantagonize the parties involved.
Criticism may reantagonize the stakeholders.

senses.definition: past tense of reantagonize; antagonized again
examples: The argument reantagonized the committee.
Their stance reantagonized the coalition.

senses.definition: present participle of reantagonize; antagonizing again
examples: The debate is reantagonizing the two groups.
His comments are reantagonizing the coalition further.

senses.definition: to create or forge again; to reconstruct or reform something (often metaphorical)
examples: The artists sought to reanvil the old sculpture with modern elements.
They aimed to reanvil the community project after the setback.

senses.definition: renewed anxiety or renewed feelings of worry
examples: After the news, a sense of reanxiety spread among the team.
The topic triggered reanxiety about future prospects.

senses.definition: to gather or harvest crops; to receive a reward or consequences as a result of actions
examples: Farmers reap the corn in autumn.
If you work hard, you will reap the benefits.
She reaped the rewards of years of study.

senses.definition: capable of being reaped or harvested; capable of yielding results
examples: Only the most mature crops are reapable.
The field is currently reapable.

senses.definition: a fictional or rare word not widely used; no standard meaning in contemporary English
examples: The term 'reapdole' does not appear in common dictionaries.

senses.definition: past tense of reap; harvested or collected as a result
examples: They reaped a rich harvest from the field.
She reaped the rewards of her hard work.
